subject: Fw: Jeffrey E.doc
body:
foward to cecil 

----- Forwarded Message ----
From: "lvjet@aol.com" <lvjet@aol.com>
To: "Montgomery, Carol L" <carol.l.montgomery@dhs.gov>
Cc: Jeffrey Epstein <jeeproject@yahoo.com>
Sent: Wednesday, June 4, 2008 11:37:23 AM
Subject: Re: Jeffrey E.doc

I received this from Carol today, she was working departures for my airline flight.
Sent from my BlackBerry® wireless handheld

-----Original Message-----
From: "Montgomery, Carol L" <carol.l.montgomery@dhs.gov>

Date: Wed, 4 Jun 2008 11:14:14 
To:<lvjet@aol.com>
Cc:"Montgomery, Carol L" <carol.l.montgomery@dhs.gov>
Subject: Jeffrey E.doc


Lawrence would you forward to Jeff for me!? It was great seeing U this morning... 
----------------


June 4, 2008 
  
Jeffrey E. Epstein, 
457 Madison Ave-4th floor 
New York, NY 10022 
  
Dear Mr. Epstein, 
  
For the past 14 years, the U.S. Customs & Border Protection Explorer Post 5101 Saint Thomas, (USCBPE) has been a youth organization, registered under the Learning for Life, a division of the Boy Scouts of America career education program, that is develop to helps young adults build character, enhance leadership, professionalism, and personal development by promoting integrity, accountability, fairness, service, and pride that is useful in selecting a career in law enforcement.   The Instructors and Advisors have joined together to work hand in hand to encourage positive behavior conducive to promoting productive, law abiding citizens amongst our youths in the community.  One of our goals is to have the community realize that the programs will also develop a positive relationship between law enforcement and the youths and citizens of this community. 
  
The advisor has completed after long hours of training and the Explorer positive service to the community would like to again this year 2007-2008 allow the recruitment the   opportunity to attend the 2008 National Law Enforcement Conference. The post consists of 25 students between the ages of 12 to 21. 
  
 The trainings are held on Saturdays at the Virgin Islands Police Academy in Subbase or at the Charlotte Amalie High School. Each Explorer is excepted to complete a minimum 100 hours of community service, attend 5 church services, complete a minimum of 100 hours of training, maintain a 70% or better average in all subjects and uphold the standards the agency and community expects of them.   
  
The Virgin Islands Police Department Training Staff and Immigration and Customs Enforcement RAC and Special Agents will be training the Explorers in the following areas: 
Accident Scene                      Traffic Stop                       Neighborhood Watch 
Bomb Treat                             Shoot, Don’t Shoot            Drill & Ceremony 
Domestic Violence                  Forensic                            Inspection 
Narcotics                                Self Defense                     White Collar Crime & Arrest Techniques 
  
  
The USCBP Explorer program mission has four specific goals. The Explorers should: 
1.   Gain practical experience in the career interest of the custom post 5101. 
2.   Engage in a program of activities centered on the five emphasis areas: 1) Career opportunities; 2) Life skills; 3) Service learning; 4) Character; 5) Education and leadership experiences. 
3.      Experience positive leadership from adult and youth leaders, given opportunities to take on leadership roles, and have a chance to learn and grow in a supportive, caring, and fun environment. 
  
I would like to ask you Jeffery if you could make any amount donation, to go towards the youth to attend the 2008 National Law Enforcement Conference.  Receiving your assistance to fund this organization will help defray the expenses that we face and will help the Advisor and Explorers to become focus on the training and  accomplishing the mission for each student to attend the conference cost is $1,300.    
  
In August or September 2008, a “Thank You” ad donated by The Daily News will be placed. Recognizing your company for the contribution you have provided.  Also, for every activity we are apart of, your company name will be displayed as a sponsor. . 
  
Documentation for tax purposes, will be issued upon request. 
  
Before making any donation, I will be happy to discuss the program some more in person or on the phone. This will allow me to answer what ever questions or concerns you may have.  My telephone number is (340) 774-1719.  However, because my location is unpredictable, you may also contact Natasha Bridgewater, CBP Explorer President .at 340-693-225.1 
  
If you feel you can make a donation, please make the check payable to U.S. Customs & Border Protection Explorer Post 5101. 
  
Your financial assistance in helping the youths of the Virgin Islands is greatly appreciated. 
  
Sincerely, 
  
  
  
Carol Montgomery 
Post 5101 Advisor 
340-998-5995 cell 
340-774-1719 Airport number
